en.extract:
The dark-throated oriole (Oriolus xanthonotus) is a species of bird in the family Oriolidae.
It is found in Southeast Asia through the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, Bangka), Java and southwestern Borneo. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests where it is threatened by habitat loss.


Taxonomy
The dark-throated oriole was formerly considered to be conspecific with the ventriloquial oriole (Oriolus consobrinus). The dark-throated oriole is sometimes considered to form a superspecies with the Philippine oriole and the Isabela oriole. Alternate names for the dark-throated oriole include the black-headed oriole, black-throated oriole and Malaysian oriole. The alternate name 'black-headed oriole' should not be confused with the species of the same name, Oriolus larvatus.
Two subspecies are recognized: 

O. x. xanthonotus - Horsfield, 1821: Found on the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, Java and south-western Borneo
O. x. mentawi - Chasen & Kloss, 1926: Found on the Mentawai Islands archipelago off the west coast of Sumatra, in Indonesia.


References

The '''dark-throated oriole''' ('''''Oriolus xanthonotus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Oriolidae]].

It is found in Southeast Asia through the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, Bangka), Java and southwestern Borneo. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s where it is threatened by [[habitat loss]].

[[File:Dark-throated Oriole (F).jpg|thumb|Dark-throated Oriole (Female)]]

==Taxonomy==
The dark-throated oriole was formerly considered to be [[conspecific]] with the [[ventriloquial oriole]] (''Oriolus consobrinus'').<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela Rasmussen | date=July 2023 | title=Orioles, drongos, fantails | work=IOC World Bird List Version 13.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/orioles/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=31 July 2023 }}</ref> The dark-throated oriole is sometimes considered to form a [[Species complex|superspecies]] with the [[Philippine oriole]] and the [[Isabela oriole]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.hbw.com/species/dark-throated-oriole-oriolus-xanthonotus|title=Dark-throated Oriole (Oriolus xanthonotus)|website=www.hbw.com|language=en|access-date=2017-02-24}}</ref> Alternate names for the dark-throated oriole include the black-headed oriole, black-throated oriole and Malaysian oriole. The alternate name 'black-headed oriole' should not be confused with the species of the same name, ''[[Oriolus larvatus]]''.

Two [[subspecies]] are recognized:<ref name=ioc/> 
* ''O. x. xanthonotus'' - <small>[[Thomas Horsfield|Horsfield]], 1821</small>: Found on the [[Malay Peninsula]], [[Sumatra]], [[Java]] and south-western [[Borneo]]
* ''O. x. mentawi'' - <small>[[Frederick Nutter Chasen|Chasen]] & [[C. Boden Kloss|Kloss]], 1926</small>: Found on the [[Mentawai Islands Regency|Mentawai Islands]] archipelago off the west coast of [[Sumatra]], in Indonesia.
